title: Soured Aerated Norwegian Chinese Cabbage Mousse
description: A delicate fusion of traditional Norwegian fermentation and Japanese mushimono techniques resulting in a light, cloud-like vegetable mousse.
instructions: Toss the shredded cabbage with salt and let it ferment in a vacuum bag for 48 hours at room temperature.
Drain the fermented cabbage thoroughly and squeeze out all excess moisture.
Puree the soured cabbage with the miso paste and heavy cream until completely smooth.
Pass the cabbage mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure a silky texture.
Whisk the egg whites in a clean bowl until they reach stiff peaks.
Gently fold the cabbage puree into the egg whites using a rubber spatula to maintain aeration.
Divide the mixture into four ceramic ramekins and cover each with foil.
Place the ramekins in a steamer basket over simmering water.
Steam the mousse gently for 12 minutes until set but still jiggly.
Garnish with crushed caraway seeds and serve immediately while warm.
